articles.summary: Houston delivered a dominant performance, beating the Los Angeles Lakers by a large margin. Victor Wembanyama led the Spurs' comeback with a standout showing, highlighting his impact after returning from injury.
key_points: Houston won convincingly, exposing Lakers' defensive lapses.
Victor Wembanyama posted a strong statistical line, signaling his return to form.
The loss adds pressure on LeBron James and the Lakers' coaching staff.

key_points: Lakers retain home‑court advantage for the second consecutive postseason.
Series opponent: Houston Rockets, a markedly different lineup than last year.
Analysts caution that the Lakers may struggle to improve on the prior series outcome.

key_points: Lakers face Game 5 without key contributors Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves.
Team is the biggest underdog among non‑eighth‑seed matchups.
Home‑court advantage may not be enough to offset injury‑related setbacks.

articles.original_title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Rockets
articles.summary: The Lakers' Game 4 effort was marred by offensive stagnation and a high number of live‑ball turnovers, which crippled any chance of victory. The analysis calls for a deeper look at the team's execution in upcoming coverage.
key_points: Offensive production stalled, described as “stuck in mud.”
Turnovers were frequent and mostly live‑ball, costing scoring opportunities.
Future analysis will focus on correcting these issues before the next game.

The win extends the series, giving Houston a chance to rebound.
Lakers must adjust to avoid a potential series turnaround.

articles.title: Wemby back, unhappy with concussion process
articles.original_title: Wemby back, unhappy with concussion process
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama had 27 points, 12 rebounds and seven blocks in his return from a concussion as the Spurs beat the Blazers for a 3-1 series lead.

articles.title: Celtics Shut Down Embiid, Seize Series Lead
articles.original_title: C's brush off Embiid's return, take control of series
articles.summary: Joel Embiid returned to boost the 76ers, but Boston's barrage of three‑point shots quickly created an insurmountable lead. The Celtics controlled the game early and took command of the series.
key_points: Embiid's return gave Philadelphia a brief spark.
Celtics hit a high volume of threes, pulling ahead early.
Boston secured a decisive win and a series advantage.

articles.title: Anthony Edwards Avoids Ligament Tear, Out for Weeks
articles.original_title: Sources: Ant avoids ligament tear, to miss time
articles.summary: Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards suffered a bone bruise and left‑knee hyperextension, but avoided a serious ligament tear. Medical sources project he will miss multiple weeks of action.
key_points: Edwards diagnosed with bone bruise and hyperextension.
No ligament tear, but recovery timeline is several weeks.
Timberwolves will be without their leading scorer for an extended period.

articles.title: Jokic, Randle Fined After Game 4 Altercation
articles.original_title: Jokic, Randle fined; no suspensions for skirmish
articles.summary: Nikola Jokic and Julius Randle were each fined for their roles in a late‑game scuffle between the Nuggets and Timberwolves. Jokic received a $50,000 fine, while Randle was fined $35,000; neither player was suspended.
key_points: Jokic fined $50,000; Randle fined $35,000.
The league opted for fines rather than suspensions.
The incident occurred at the end of Game 4 between Denver and Minnesota.

articles.title: Raptors Advance Over Cavs Despite Historic 3‑Point Drought
articles.original_title: Raps top Cavs despite historically cold shooting
articles.summary: Toronto defeated Cleveland in Game 4, even though the Raptors shot just 13.3% from three‑point range—the lowest ever in a playoff win. The victory moved the series forward despite the poor shooting night.
key_points: Raptors set a playoff record with a 13.3% three‑point shooting percentage.
They still managed to outlast the Cavaliers and win Game 4.
The win gives Toronto a series edge heading into the next matchup.

Kevin Durant continues to be unavailable due to injury.

articles.title: LeBron James reaches 3,000 career playoff field goals
articles.original_title: LeBron James recorded his 3,000th career playoff field … - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: LeBron James became the first player in NBA history to hit 3,000 field goals in the postseason, cementing his legacy as the all‑time playoff scoring leader. The milestone came during the Lakers' recent playoff series.
key_points: 3,000th playoff field goal makes LeBron the all‑time leader.
Milestone achieved in a Lakers playoff game.
Further solidifies LeBron’s case for greatest‑of‑all‑time discussions.

Starters, including LeBron, earned mixed grades due to efficiency issues.

The backlash reflects the intense scrutiny LeBron faces each game.

articles.summary: Post‑game, LeBron James took responsibility for the turnovers he committed during the loss to the Rockets, emphasizing the need for better ball handling and decision‑making moving forward. His comments signal a focus on accountability within the team.
key_points: LeBron publicly acknowledged his turnover count.
He stressed the importance of limiting mistakes in future games.
The admission points to a leadership approach aimed at improving team performance.

articles.title: Rockets Rout Lakers 115-96
articles.original_title: Rockets 115-96 Lakers (Apr 26, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN
articles.summary: The Houston Rockets defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 115-96 on April 26, 2026. The win was highlighted by dominant scoring runs and strong defensive play that limited LeBron James and the Lakers' offense.
key_points: Houston posted a 19-point victory, holding Los Angeles to just 96 points.
The Rockets' balanced attack featured multiple players in double figures.
LeBron James struggled to find rhythm, contributing below his season averages.
